wizard.title: Register

validation.smurfTagsRequired: Add at least one smurf account.

accounts.title: Your Accounts
accounts.desc: We've pre-filled your linked accounts. Change them if needed.
accounts.verifiedHint: Choose one of your OAuth-verified accounts.

roles.title: Choose Your Role
roles.desc: Set your primary role first. Then choose secondary roles we can assign you to if a team is short on them during balancing.

primary.title: Primary Role
primary.desc: This is the role we should place you on by default.

secondary.title: Secondary Roles
secondary.descEmptyPrimary: Pick a primary role first to reveal your fallback role options.
secondary.descFlex: Flex already covers all roles, so no fallback roles are needed.
secondary.descFallback: These are fallback roles. If a team is missing one of them, we may place you there during balancing.
secondary.emptyStateFlex: Flex already covers every role, so fallback roles are not needed for this registration.
secondary.emptyStateChoosePrimary: Choose a primary role first. We will then show the two fallback roles you can also be assigned to.

topHeroes.title: Top Heroes
topHeroes.desc: Pick up to {max} hero(es) you play best for each role.

details.title: Additional Details
details.descWithFields: Almost done! Fill in any extra info.
details.descNoFields: No additional fields required. You're ready to submit!
details.notesPlaceholder: Anything you'd like organizers to know

rankAutofill.mismatchOnlyAria: Show only players whose current rank differs from the suggestion
rankAutofill.noTournamentDescription: Choose a tournament in the sidebar before configuring rank autofill.

verdict.forecast: forecast off by ~{delta} places on average

triage.title: Needs attention

deepDive.title: Deep dive

page.chooseParamsDesc: Select a tournament and an algorithm to view analytics.
page.unavailableDesc: Failed to load analytics for the selected parameters.
page.noTeamsDesc: No teams found for the selected tournament.

horizon.title: Predicted vs actual horizon
horizon.subtitle: Open ring is predicted. Filled dot is actual placement.

distribution.title: Predicted standings distribution
distribution.subtitle: Monte Carlo simulation (5000 iter) over the calibrated pairwise win-probability model.
distribution.unavailable: Standings v2 not available. Run the trainer first.
distribution.noData: No predictions yet for this tournament.

matchQuality.title: Match quality & anomalies
matchQuality.subtitle: Post-hoc per-encounter scoring (how close, how expected, how even) plus anomaly flags.
matchQuality.unavailable: Match Quality not available. Run the inference pipeline first.

anomalyLegend.title: Anomaly flags
anomalyLegend.note: Flags are review hints, not verdicts.

anomalyReason.top_impact: Among the strongest performers in their role
anomalyReason.low_rank: Registered at a low rank for results this strong
anomalyReason.cohort_overperformance: Outplayed others of the same role and division
anomalyReason.strong_cohort_outlier: Far above peers of the same role and division
anomalyReason.raw_mvp_dominance: Consistently tops the match scoreboard (MVP) for their rank
anomalyReason.single_tournament_underperformance: Played well below their level this tournament
anomalyReason.sustained_underperformance: Consistently below their level across recent tournaments
anomalyReason.sharp_recent_drop: A sharp drop from their usual results

explanation.title: What drove this score
explanation.subtitle: The stats that pushed it above or below the average for this role.

confidence.label: Confidence
confidence.plain: How sure the model is about this forecast, based on how much history and match data it had.

shift.label: Division adjustment
shift.plain: Suggested change to a player's division for the next tournament (＋ moves up, − moves down).

impact.label: Impact
impact.plain: How much better or worse the player did than expected for the match-up — 0–100 within their role.

vs_local.label: vs similar players
vs_local.plain: Performance compared to players of the same role and nearby division (0 = average, ＋ above, − below).

points.label: Move signal
points.plain: The raw signal driving the division forecast — the larger it is, the stronger the up/down push.

recent_moves.label: Recent moves
recent_moves.plain: Division changes in the player's last two tournaments.

forecast_place.label: Forecast place
forecast_place.plain: Where the model expects the team to finish.

likely_range.label: Likely finish
likely_range.plain: Where the team lands in 80% of simulated tournaments (10th–90th percentile).

prob_top.label: Chance for top finish
prob_top.plain: Share of simulated tournaments where the team finished in the top N.

competitiveness.label: How close
competitiveness.plain: How back-and-forth the match was — 100 is a nail-biter, 0 is a blowout.

predictability.label: How expected
predictability.plain: How well the result matched the pre-match forecast — 100 means it went as expected.

skill_balance.label: How even
skill_balance.plain: How evenly matched the two teams' ratings were — 100 is a perfectly even match-up.

match_quality.label: Match quality
match_quality.plain: Overall watchability: a blend of how close, how expected and how even the match was.

forecast_miss.label: Forecast miss
forecast_miss.plain: How far the forecast landed from the real finish, in places.

evidence.label: Evidence
evidence.plain: Weighted amount of history behind the forecast — more evidence means more confidence.

newcomer.label: Newcomer
newcomer.plain: Playing their first tournament, or their first time in this role.

why_score.label: Why this score
why_score.plain: The player stats that pushed the impact score up or down the most.

smurf.label: Possible smurf
smurf.plain: Playing well above their division — could be a strong player on an under-ranked account.

throw.label: Possible throw
throw.plain: Looks like they stopped trying mid-series — play dropped sharply below their own usual level.

troll.label: Possible griefing
troll.plain: Repeatedly playing below their own level, dragging their team down.

sandbag.label: Possible sandbag
sandbag.plain: One unexpectedly weak tournament that stands out against the player's own track record.

climbing.label: Climbing
climbing.plain: Players the model thinks are playing above their division and should move up a tier.

dropping.label: Dropping
dropping.plain: Players performing below their division — the model leans toward moving them down a tier.

watch.label: Watch flags
watch.plain: Automatic heads-ups when someone's results look unusual. A prompt for a human to look — never a verdict.

avg_confidence.label: Average confidence
avg_confidence.plain: How much data is behind the calls across the whole bracket. It climbs as more maps are played.

upsets.label: Upsets
upsets.plain: Teams that finished four or more places away from their predicted spot — the bracket's biggest surprises.

new_faces.label: New faces
new_faces.plain: Players new to the tournament, or playing a role they haven't before. Their numbers carry less weight until we see more.

predicted_move.label: Predicted move
predicted_move.plain: Whether the model thinks a player belongs in a higher (climb) or lower (drop) division than they're in now. Hold means they're right where they should be.

impact_percentile.label: Impact percentile
impact_percentile.plain: Where a player's impact ranks among others in the same role and division — 90 means they beat almost everyone at their level.

verdict.story: {team} are the story — the model had them {predicted}, they're sitting {place}.
verdict.letdown: Meanwhile {team} are the bracket's let-down, slipping from a predicted {predicted} to {place}.
verdict.empty: Too early to call — no team has strayed far from its forecast yet.

standings.title: Standings
standings.watchEmpty: No watch flags right now — the bracket's clean.

team.pickPrompt: Pick a team to dig into its roster.

player.flagBody: Automatically raised for review — not a verdict. Tap the ⓘ to see what "{kind}" means.

why.flag: Flagged as {kind} — the model is holding its forecast until it sees more.
why.climbImpact: Top-tier impact among Div {division} {role}s this event.
why.climbConfidence: The model is {pct}% confident — enough evidence to suggest a climb.
why.dropConsistent: Consistent across {maps} maps, so the model leans toward a drop.
why.holdInBand: Performing about where a Div {division} player should — no move expected.
why.holdThin: Sample is still thin, so confidence is only {pct}%.
why.holdSteady: Steady, in-band results across the bracket.

howItWorks.title: Reading the analytics
howItWorks.intro: After every map, the model re-rates every player and team. This page turns that into three simple questions:
howItWorks.step1Body: The verdict up top names the team that beat its predicted finish by the most — and the one that fell short.
howItWorks.step2Body: Each player gets a predicted move — the model's read on whether they're playing above or below their current division.
howItWorks.step3Body: Confidence bars show how much data is behind each call. Tap any dotted term or the ⓘ to see what it means.
howItWorks.foot: Nothing here is final — it's a live read that sharpens as the bracket plays out.
howItWorks.cardTitle: New here? Read the analytics in 30s
howItWorks.cardSubtitle: What every number on this page means.

compare.title: Users Compare
compare.subtitle: Compare overall performance on a hero/map.

guide.heading: How to use
guide.step1: Pick your player in the Selected user field.
guide.step2: In Compare against, pick a user for a head-to-head, or clear the selection to compare against the all-players average.
guide.step3: When you select a target user, the division filters clear automatically and the comparison switches to target-user mode.
guide.step4: Choose a scope: Overall performance or Hero/Map performance.
guide.step5: In Hero/Map, the primary hero syncs the compare hero; changing the compare hero leaves the primary untouched.
guide.step6: For the all-players average, you can narrow the baseline with the role/division filters.
